_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-24 ETCO2 Monitoring (Revised 2/13/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Procedure (All EMS Levels — scope varies by modality)
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-24: ETCO2 Monitoring — MDHHS Approved 2/13/2023
purpose: Govern end-tidal CO2 (ETCO2) monitoring in prehospital EMS. Waveform capnography is mandatory for ETT confirmation and required for all transport ventilators and critically ill patients. Colorimetric capnometry is acceptable for MFR/BLS confirmation. ETCO2 = 0 with advanced airway mandates removal of the airway if not correctable.

sections.title: Mandatory ETCO2 Requirements
points: WAVEFORM CAPNOGRAPHY is MANDATORY for endotracheal tube (ETT) confirmation of placement
CAPNOGRAPHY or CAPNOMETRY is REQUIRED for ALL supraglottic airway (SGA) devices to confirm placement
WAVEFORM CAPNOGRAPHY MUST be used with ALL transport ventilators
WAVEFORM CAPNOGRAPHY MUST be used for all critically ill patients AND all ROSC patients transported at ALS or LALS level
Colorimetric capnometry is acceptable for MFR and BLS level airway confirmation
Numeric capnometry is preferred over colorimetric when available

sections.title: Provider Scope
points: EMT: colorimetric capnometry; numeric capnometry; waveform capnography per MCA selection
Specialist: waveform capnography authorized (in addition to colorimetric and numeric)

sections.title: ETCO2 = 0 or No Waveform
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-24: ETCO2 — Zero Reading Protocol
points: If ETCO2 reads 0 or waveform is absent:
STEP 1: Ensure adequate CPR quality (rate 100–120/min; adequate depth; full recoil)
STEP 2: Ensure adequate ventilation (check BVM seal, rate, volume)
STEP 3: Ensure adequate circulation (check pulse, address reversible causes in arrest)
STEP 4: Verify tubing and monitor connections — check for disconnections, kinks, moisture in line
STEP 5: If an advanced airway is in place AND ETCO2 remains 0 after the above steps → REMOVE the advanced airway immediately and manage airway manually (BVM + basic adjuncts)
Do NOT leave an advanced airway in place with a sustained ETCO2 of 0

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether EMT-level providers are authorized to use waveform capnography (numeric capnometry is authorized for EMTs by default without this selection)
options: Not authorized at EMT level — Specialist and above only

deterioration_flags: ETCO2 = 0 with advanced airway — ensure adequate CPR/ventilation/circulation; check tubing; if still 0 with ETT/SGA → REMOVE airway immediately and ventilate manually
Sustained low ETCO2 (< 10 mmHg) during CPR — reassess CPR quality; consider reversible causes
ETCO2 suddenly drops to near 0 from higher value during CPR — may indicate cardiac arrest from a reversible cause; reassess rhythm and check for ROSC
scope_notes: ETT placement: WAVEFORM CAPNOGRAPHY mandatory (not colorimetric alone)
SGA placement: capnography or capnometry required
Critically ill patients and ROSC at ALS/LALS: waveform capnography required
EMT: colorimetric + numeric; waveform per MCA selection
Specialist and Paramedic: waveform capnography authorized
ETCO2 = 0 with advanced airway in place after troubleshooting → REMOVE airway
